condensed_reasoning:
## Research

I dispatched a single research sub-agent to determine the format status (direct vs. indirect) of US-Iran nuclear negotiations and whether any direct, in-person round was scheduled for the July 11-23, 2026 window. The sub-agent hit some tool friction — AskNews was out of credits (403), and WebFetch was blocked (451/403) on CNN, Al Jazeera, Axios, and Wikipedia — but `web_search`, `search_exa`, and a few successful WebFetches (NPR, Arms Control Association, The National) gave me a rich, well-sourced picture through July 12-13.

## Key Evidence

- **Status quo is INDIRECT.** All of 2026's technical track ran through Qatari, Pakistani, and Omani mediators. Iran repeatedly and explicitly refused direct talks — FM spokesman Baqaei said Iran had `"no plans for negotiations with the American side at any level"` — while the US (VP Vance) dismissed Iran's denials as `"a Persian negotiating tactic."`
- **A direct round WAS expected inside the window.** Anadolu (Jul 2) and Dawn/Independent Pakistan (Jul 5) reported officials expected **high-level DIRECT talks in Doha in the "third week of July" (~Jul 13-19)**, with technical talks resuming ~July 11 (Islamabad front-runner venue). Pre-escalation, this would have made YES near coin-flip.
- **A major escalation Jul 7-13 derailed the track.** Trump declared the ceasefire `"OVER"` (Jul 8); CENTCOM struck **300+ targets over three nights**; on **July 12 Iran attacked five Gulf states including mediators Qatar and Oman**, declared the **Strait of Hormuz closed**, denied even requesting US talks, and said it won't negotiate under attack. Chief negotiator Qalibaf: `"The era of one-sided deals is OVER."` The only near-term contact mechanism reported was a **mediated multi-party phone call**, not an in-person meeting.
- **Precedent for direct rounds exists** in this series (Islamabad April: 2 of 3 rounds direct; Lucerne June: "some direct talks"), and the fine print counts ceasefire negotiations — leaving room for a high-level direct circuit-breaker under 60-day MoU deadline pressure.

## Conclusion

I framed this as a conjunction: resolving YES requires (1) de-escalation enough for talks to resume AND (2) those talks being in the **DIRECT** face-to-face format Iran has consistently refused, all within a shrinking ~10-day window. I built five factors (status-quo indirect refusal −1.3; pre-escalation direct-Doha expectation +1.3; dominant Jul 7-13 escalation −1.6; direct-round precedent +0.7; high-bar-during-bombing −0.4). The additive factor-implied probability came out ~22%, but I judged that the required conjunction justifies going below the additive sum, landing at **~13%** (logit −1.9). My reflection anchored on a ~15-20% base rate for a format-shift event, and my premortem constructed a coherent 25-35% YES case (refusal-as-posture, war-driven ceasefire contact) with a 0.62 confidence self-assessment. The premortem verifier approved with an independent ~12-15% estimate. **Final estimate ~13%.**

capability_gaps: No prediction-market lookup (Polymarket/Kalshi/Metaculus) was attempted; a market on US-Iran talks could have provided an external anchor for this volatile scenario.
AskNews tier/credit limits removed a potentially useful high-volume news aggregator; the agent had no equivalent high-recall news tool as backup beyond web_search/exa.
subtle_bugs: Several WebFetch calls returned only page metadata/boilerplate (e.g., The National article) rather than article body, which could have produced a false 'content unavailable' impression; the agent correctly recognized this and did not over-infer from the empty shells.

classification.approach: Qualitative factor-based logit aggregation over news research on US-Iran talk format and scheduling, framed as a conjunction of de-escalation AND direct format within a 10-day window.
classification.key_uncertainty: Whether a sudden ceasefire materializes within the window and, if so, whether resumed talks take the direct same-room format Iran has consistently refused rather than reverting to indirect/mediated contact.

risk_flags.evidence: The agent treats YES as requiring a same-room bilateral session and correctly excludes the mediated phone call. But it never states the exact resolution text and does not fully resolve whether a quadrilateral Lucerne-style format (US and Iran both physically present with mediators) or ceasefire-focused direct contact would count as 'direct, in-person talks,' leaving the direct/quadrilateral boundary ambiguous.

The agent's own factor-aggregation tool produced an implied probability of ~22% and its reflection anchor was 15-20%, yet it manually pushed the final estimate down to 13% citing the conjunction. Given genuine pre-escalation momentum toward a direct Doha round inside the window and Iran's history of refusal-as-posture, the discretionary downward adjustment from ~22% to 13% may be somewhat aggressive.

strengths: Correctly framed the question as a conjunction (de-escalation AND direct format), which is the crux — many forecasters would have anchored only on 'will talks resume' and missed the format requirement.

Surfaced the key tension explicitly: a direct Doha round was expected in the exact window (Anadolu Jul 2, Dawn Jul 5) BEFORE the Jul 7-13 escalation reversed it, giving both the upward and downward pulls their proper weight.
Applied the resolution distinction well — correctly ruling out the mediated multi-party phone call as not qualifying for 'in-person same-room' talks.
Recognized that the additive factor sum (~22%) overstates probability for a conjunctive event and adjusted downward with stated reasoning rather than mechanically reporting the factor output.
Premortem constructed a genuine, quantified counterargument (25-35% YES band) with a concrete falsification trigger rather than a token devil's-advocate.
weaknesses: Never quoted the exact resolution criteria text, leaving ambiguity about whether a quadrilateral/mediated-but-co-located meeting (like Lucerne) or ceasefire-arranging direct contact would count as 'direct, in-person talks.'

Relied entirely on one research sub-agent call; given the same-day close and fast-moving war, a second targeted pull specifically on July 13 developments (was any face-to-face contact confirmed?) would have tightened the near-term read.

The discretionary jump from the tool's ~22% factor-implied probability and the 15-20% base-rate anchor down to 13% is under-justified — the magnitude of the conjunction penalty is asserted rather than derived.

Did not decompose the two conjunctive probabilities explicitly (P(de-escalation in window) × P(direct format | talks resume)), which would have made the final number more defensible than a single logit adjustment.

issues: AskNews returned 403 tier-restriction and credit-overage errors on all 3 attempts, wasting calls early in the research sub-agent.
WebFetch was blocked (451/403) on CNN, Al Jazeera, Axios, and Wikipedia — 4 of 6 fetches failed — forcing reliance on search-tool snippets.
Some large Grep outputs in the premortem sub-agent spilled to persisted-output files (52KB/98KB), mild token/attention overhead but not blocking.
